note: Maps each checkpoint's native policy scope onto the shared binary top-level task. Mechanical mapping to a shared binary label does NOT make the source ontologies or native policy scopes identical; those differences stay explicit here (proposal Section 8.1). The binary manifest carries no native categories, hazard IDs, Controversial labels, or response/refusal labels, so no auxiliary schema is improvised as a training target.

shared_binary_task.definition: unsafe = harmful content, a jailbreak, or a prompt injection (Paper A binary top-level task)

shieldgemma_2b.study_native_target: Yes|No under one locked broad study policy
shieldgemma_2b.official_native_target: policy_conditioned_yes_no over four policies with locked maximum-risk aggregation
shieldgemma_2b.mapping: study_policy_subsumes_binary_unsafe; four native policies NOT reconstructable as four training targets from one binary label
shieldgemma_2b.shieldgemma_study_policy_note: Primary adaptation uses ONE locked broad study policy because the binary manifest cannot reconstruct labels for the four separate official vendor policies; the four advertised policies remain an official-native evaluation panel only, never a training target.

qwen3guard_gen_06b.mapping: binary unsafe -> Unsafe decision position; Controversial and categories masked from CE and measured as retention

qwen3guard_gen_4b.mapping: binary unsafe -> Unsafe decision position; Controversial and categories masked from CE and measured as retention

granite_guardian_31_2b.mapping: binary unsafe -> Yes under the general-harm risk definition

llama_guard_3_1b.official_native_target: official single-user chat template full safe/unsafe verdict; hazards as diagnostics
llama_guard_3_1b.mapping: binary unsafe -> unsafe verdict; absent hazard IDs never falsely supervised

wildguard_7b.official_native_target: full official structured prompt; prompt-harm field scored for prompt-only evaluation
wildguard_7b.mapping: binary unsafe -> prompt-harm=yes; response/refusal fields masked from CE, generation measured as retention; missing fields never default to safe

scope_caveats: native policy scopes differ across checkpoints; study-native averages inherit a contract-mapping limitation
a general guard's miss on an out-of-scope policy is not automatically a model failure
official-native and study-native panels are never pooled
